entry.title: Where ORD Is Used in SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud & SAP Databricks
entry.content:
SAP Open Resource Discovery (ORD) is becoming a foundational standard across the SAP ecosystem because it provides a consistent, self‑describing metadata layer for APIs, events, business objects, and data products. This metadata standardization is what enables smooth interoperability across SAP’s modern data and integration platforms.

Below are the key places where ORD is used today — and how it empowers seamless integration, especially with SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud, and SAP Databricks.

1. SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com)

SAP Business Accelerator Hub uses ORD as one of the primary mechanisms for publishing APIs and events. Any API showing an ORD ID on its overview page is officially ORD-enabled, meaning it exposes standardized metadata that can be consumed by SAP tools and data platforms.

This ensures:

  • Consistent API descriptions
  • Discoverability by SAP’s central aggregators
  • Compatibility with downstream systems like SAP Business Data Cloud
This is the first indicator that an API can participate in the broader data product ecosystem. 
 

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

SAP uses ORD internally to improve SAP-to-SAP integration and provide a unified metadata layer that development tools (like SAP Business Application Studio) can consume for enhanced API discovery and automation.

Each Product Owner from SAP Products LoB will be develop, design ORD to make it available in SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com) and Data Products Marketplace for you..

3. SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)

SAP Business Data Cloud actively uses ORD to describe Data Products and make them discoverable to consumers across the SAP ecosystem, including SAP Datasphere. ORD helps BDC deliver a unified catalog with:

  • Descriptions of datasets
  • Relationships
  • Capabilities
  • Higher-level metadata structures like data products and business entities
In addition, third‑party systems such as SAP Databricks are required to describe data products using ORD before publishing them into SAP Business Data Cloud.
 
This ensures smooth catalog integration and consistent metadata across SAP and non-SAP data sources.
 

4. SAP Datasphere

SAP Datasphere consumes data products published to SAP Business Data Cloud — and because BDC requires ORD‑based metadata, Datasphere gains:

  • Automatically discoverable data products
  • Standardized metadata for modeling
  • Compatibility with external data such as Databricks Delta Lake

Datasphere uses ORD indirectly because it relies on BDC’s ORD-driven catalog for understanding and federating data assets.

Additionally, SAP’s hybrid integration patterns show that Datasphere can integrate with Databricks using JDBC or federated models — and ORD ensures data products between these systems are consistently described.

5. SAP Databricks (Enterprise Databricks Integration)

SAP Databricks integrates deeply with SAP Business Data Cloud using BDC Connect, enabling bi-directional sharing of data products. To publish a data product from SAP Databricks to SAP Business Data Cloud:

  • The metadata must be described using ORD and CSN (Core Schema Notation)
  • The data product becomes discoverable to SAP systems (e.g., Datasphere) via BDC

Furthermore, Databricks–SAP integration uses Delta Share, JDBC, and federated models, but ORD plays the critical role of standardizing data product metadata.

This creates a smooth, unified integration path:

SAP applications → ORD-based metadata → Business Data Cloud → Datasphere & Databricks

SAP Architecture Center also confirms this hybrid pattern where business data from SAP and non‑SAP (Databricks) is harmonized through Business Data Cloud and Datasphere, enabling interoperable AI/ML and analytics scenarios.

 Refer my SAP blog

6. Data Products Across the SAP Ecosystem

ORD also provides standardized metadata for:

  • Entity Types / Business Objects
  • APIs and Events
  • Data Products 
This consistency is the key enabler for cross‑platform interoperability — especially as Data Products become central to the SAP ecosystem via Business Data Cloud and Datasphere.

Summary: How ORD Enables Smooth Integration Across SAP & Databricks

With ORD in place:

  • APIs with an ORD ID on api.sap.com can be safely consumed to build data products in SAP Business Data Cloud.
  • Business Data Cloud uses ORD metadata to catalog and serve data products to SAP Datasphere and SAP Databricks.
  • SAP Databricks uses ORD when publishing its own data products back into Business Data Cloud.
  • SAP Datasphere leverages ORD-driven metadata to discover, model, and federate data products seamlessly.

ORD is the metadata “glue” that ensures smooth integration across:

SAP BTP → Business Accelerator Hub → Business Data Cloud → Datasphere → Databricks

And the more SAP and partner solutions adopt ORD, the smoother, more automated, and more scalable integrations become across the SAP landscape.

entry.title: Customize SAPUI5 apps for business users – make an impact by joining our CEI project
entry.content:
SAP Customer Engagement Initiatives (CEI) on https://influence.sap.com allow you, as an SAP customer or partner, to gain early insights into SAP's product developments and work directly with SAP teams to shape future product directions. This project presents a unique opportunity to work with SAPUI5 flexibility product managers and engineering teams, leveraging your expertise customizing SAPUI5 apps with key user adaptation.

Join the upcoming CEI project to make an impact on how key users interact with key user adaptation

If you’re already convinced – join here. If not, read on 🙂

What is key user adaptation?

Key user adaptation is a mode that allows extending SAPUI5 applications for other users right in the browser. It is highly intuitive and requires no technical or programming skills. Just use drag and drop to make UI changes – no modification or copy of SAP standard coding necessary! Read more here.

What is the targeted improvement?

Although the product is mature and widely used, we actively evolve it by delivering new functionality and capability enhancements alongside continuous user experience improvements, informed by UI testing and direct customer feedback. 

We are in the process of overhauling key user adaptation’s UI, simplifying the overall approach while making often-used scenarios more streamlined. We’ve taken a close look at the version history (of activated adaptations) and how our users interact with it, extended the “embed third-party content” feature, widened the scope of custom fields that can be added to the UI – and more.

To ensure that we are on the right way, we need your help!

Who should participate?

Users who want to adapt standard SAPUI5 apps for business users. Both key users and technical users are welcome.

Why should you participate?

  • Gain understanding of SAPUI5 flexibility
  • Chance to influence a central SAPUI5 feature
  • Early access to prototypes

How can you participate?

Don’t miss out on this opportunity to share your feedback and engage with SAPUI5 flexibility development teams. Register by March 15, 2026, and make a true impact on how you can customize SAP apps to your needs!

Click here to sign up for our project. (A short overview about how to get started on influence.sap.com can be found here).

After you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call where you will get more information. This call is followed by individual or grouped feedback sessions.

Thank you in advance for helping us shape the future of SAPUI5 flexibility!

entry.title: Running SAP AMS Like a Product, Not a Support Factory
entry.content:
Running SAP AMS Like a Product, Not a Support Factory

Introduction

In many S/4HANA environments, the real challenge begins after go-live, during stabilisation. This blog shares a practical experience from a healthcare SAP landscape where SLA compliance was strong, yet recurring issues were eroding business confidence. I will outline what changed when we shifted focus from ticket closure to process-level stability.

A few years ago, I was supporting a large healthcare and pharmaceutical organisation in India. The SAP landscape was not small. Multiple company codes, manufacturing plants, finance, MM, SD, and integration touchpoints. At peak, the AMS team was handling close to  70 to 90 incidents per week across modules.

On paper, we were stable. SLAs were green. Response times were acceptable. Escalations were limited.

But stability is not the same as confidence.

Three weeks after one S/4HANA go-live stabilisation cycle, finance users began reporting repeated FI posting failures during month-end close. The same issue was logged again. And again. And again.

Over a two-week period, the same posting issue surfaced repeatedly, close to 8 to 10 times, mostly during month-end processing.

Each ticket was resolved within SLA. Each one was technically “closed.”
And yet, nothing was actually fixed.

From a dashboard perspective, AMS looked healthy. From a business perspective, frustration was building.

I still remember one escalation call. A senior finance stakeholder asked:

“If we are closing tickets, why does this keep coming back during month-end?”

That question was uncomfortable. Because the honest answer was ,we were optimizing for closure, not stability.

What Was Really Happening

The root cause turned out to be a configuration gap combined with a background job timing conflict. Nothing dramatic. No major design flaw. Just a gap that slipped through during hypercare.

But the way we were operating AMS made it worse.

Incidents were assigned by queue. Functional would resolve one aspect. Technical would adjust something. The ticket would close. And ownership would dissolve.

Two weeks later, month-end would expose the same weakness.

We had documentation. We had RCA notes. What we did not have was single-point accountability for eliminating recurrence.

And that is where I realised this was not a technical issue. It was an operating model issue.

The Shift

We paused low-priority enhancement work for that module. Not popular at the time.

We grouped recurring incidents instead of treating them individually. Assigned one accountable lead for the entire FI process area. Not for a ticket for the outcome.

We introduced a simple stability metric: repeat incident count per process area. Not just SLA compliance.

Repeat FI posting incidents during month-end reduced by approximately 40 to 60% over the next two stabilisation cycles..

Total weekly incident volume across SAP reduced gradually as patterns were eliminated rather than recycled.

More importantly, escalation tone changed. Instead of: “Why is this failing again?”

The conversation shifted to: ‘What risks are we proactively addressing before next month-end?’

That was a turning point.

What I Learned

For a long time, I believed strong SLA governance meant strong AMS. I defended green dashboards in meetings. In hindsight, I was defending the wrong metric.

Ticket closure is activity. Stability is outcome.

Running SAP AMS like a product means:

  • Clear ownership at process level
  • Measuring recurrence, not just response time
  • Prioritising structural fixes over cosmetic closure
  • Accepting short-term delivery pressure to gain long-term stability

It does not require heavy transformation. It requires clarity and discipline.

I have seen this pattern more than once , especially in healthcare and pharma environments where compliance and month-end accuracy are critical.

The lesson for me was simple.

If the same issue returns, the system is telling you something.
Dashboards may stay green. But business memory does not reset every month.

That experience fundamentally changed how I lead AMS today.

If you are running SAP AMS in a post-S/4HANA environment, I would be curious:

Are you measuring how fast tickets close or how rarely they return?

 

Conclusion

Stabilisation after S/4HANA go-live is not only about meeting SLAs. It is about reducing recurrence and rebuilding business confidence. By shifting focus from ticket closure to process-level accountability, we were able to improve stability in a measurable way. For organisations running SAP AMS today, the real question is not how fast incidents close, but whether the same ones continue to return.

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone: Tile Opens(content federation) but App Fails with ‘IP Address Cannot Be Found
entry.content:
In my SAP Build Work Zone (Standard Edition) environment, I faced an issue where a dynamic tile loaded successfully through content federation, but clicking the tile caused the application to fail with a browser error:

ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED – The IP address of the server could not be found

The failing URL always contained a very long auto‑generated hostname.

 

Interestingly, the dynamic tile itself worked correctly (e.g., OData count displayed), but navigating to the corresponding SAP Fiori app consistently failed. This clearly indicated that the issue was not with backend connectivity or destinations, but with the content runtime URL generated by Work Zone.
 
Please refer the screenshots.

entry.title: How ATP & Advanced ATP (aATP) Supercharge the SAP CPQ Sales Quotation Process
entry.content:
When sales teams prepare quotes, one of the biggest challenges is determining accurate product availability and realistic delivery dates. SAP CPQ excels at configuration, pricing, and quote generation, but to promise delivery with confidence, it must rely on real-time inventory and supply chain insights.

That is exactly where SAP ATP (Available‑to‑Promise) and SAP aATP (Advanced Available‑to‑Promise) become powerful extensions during Sales process purely designed and developed custom Iflow (Not available from SAP CPQ Standard Integration Packages).

This blog explores how ATP/aATP works, how it integrates with SAP CPQ and S/4HANA, and the tangible benefits it brings to the sales quotation cycle.

Available Standard APIs for S/4 HANA Public/Private/Onprem

Standard APIsS/4 HANA Public CloudS/4 HANA OnPrem(Private Cloud)
Classical ATPProduct Availability InfoProduct Availability Info
 Basic Product Availability Info
Basic Product Availability Info
 Advanced Backorder Processing Run  
Advanced ATPAdvanced ATP Check Advanced ATP Check
 Supply Availability Protection Plan  
 Product Allocation Object  
 Product Substitution Control
 
 Product Substitution Group
 
 Plant Substitution Control  
 Storage Location Substitution
 
 Storage Location Substitution Exclusion  
 Storage Location Substitution Group  

 

1. What Is ATP and aATP?

ATP – Classic Availability Check

ATP checks evaluate whether requested materials are available on a given date based on stock, incoming supply, and existing commitments. Classic ATP is embedded in SAP ERP and S/4HANA

aATP – Advanced, Intelligent Availability Checking

Advanced ATP in S/4HANA brings enhanced capabilities, including:

  • Product Availability Check (PAC)
  • Backorder Processing (BOP)
  • Product Allocation (PAL)
  • Alternative-Based Confirmation (ABC)
    These provide intelligent, real-time confirmations considering multiple locations, constraints, and rules

2. Why ATP/aATP Matters for SAP CPQ

SAP CPQ can generate highly accurate quotes, but without visibility into inventory or supply constraints, quotes may overpromise and damage customer trust. ATP/aATP ensures:

  • Real-time material availability during quoting
  • Accurate delivery dates
  • Feasible commitments, even with complex configurations
  • Optimized supply allocation for high-value customers

As SAP CPQ integrates natively with SAP S/4HANA via custom development, sales reps can access availability checks via APIs such as API_AVAIL_TO_PROMISE_CHECK or during quotation-to-sales-order conversion.

3. How ATP/aATP Integrates with SAP CPQ

3.1 Custom Development SAP CPQ–S/4HANA Integration

SAP provides predefined CPQ integration with S/4HANA via BTP Cloud Integration, enabling exchange of configuration, pricing, and master data. A custom Iflow needs to be designed, developed and to be tested to your business requirments that requires ATP or aATP during the Sales Quotation Process before the Sales Order/Solution Quotation/Solution Order is been triggerred

Once CPQ creates a quote and the user triggers an ATP check (either directly or when converting the quote to an order), S/4HANA performs:

  • Real-time availability check
  • Temporary quantity assignment (TQA)
  • Confirmation simulation
    These ATP checks help prevent false confirmations and maintain accuracy in early phase quotations.

 

3.2 ATP Simulation in the CPQ Process

Sales Order supports ATP simulation via OData APIs, allowing CPQ to request real-time availability before final order creation. 

3.3 Variant Configuration & ATP

When quotes involve configurable products, the integration leverages SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing, ensuring ATP checks consider(This is not part of the Custom Iflow Development):

  • Component availability
  • Alternative sourcing options
  • Production and supply constraints

4. Benefits of Integrating ATP/aATP with SAP CPQ

4.1 Accurate, Real-Time Quotes

aATP delivers real-time confirmations using S/4HANA’s in-memory performance, ensuring CPQ quotes reflect live supply chain data.

4.2 Reduced Order Rejections

By validating availability early, CPQ avoids orders failing later due to material shortages or unrealistic dates.

4.3 Improved Customer Commitment Accuracy

With ABC, BOP, and PAL, CPQ can provide delivery promises aligned with supply chain realities, reducing missed delivery windows.

4.5 Enhanced Sales Productivity

Sales reps can generate quotes more confidently without waiting for manual supply chain consultations.
SAP CPQ already accelerates complex quote creation, and ATP enhances accuracy

4.4 Optimized Fulfillment Decisions

aATP can automatically evaluate multiple warehouses, alternative plants, or substitute products before CPQ confirms availability.

 

Conclusion

SAP ATP/aATP transforms SAP CPQ from a powerful quoting tool into an end-to-end, availability-aware commitment system.
By combining CPQ’s configuration and pricing intelligence with real-time supply checks, organizations achieve:

  • Faster, more accurate quotations
  • Stronger order reliability
  • Greater customer satisfaction
  • Optimized supply chain execution

In a world where customers expect instant, accurate promises, integrating ATP/aATP with SAP CPQ is no longer optional—it’s a competitive necessity.

entry.title: Tips while configuring SAP Asset accounting and Data upload.
entry.content:
Recently i got a chance to work in 1 project where my role was of Asset Data migration lead. I had to monitor the uploads of the Asset Data.  In each project i learn something and i document it for future reference. I convert that document into blog and share with my community so that they also can benefit from it. 

Disclaimer - This blog does not reflect my expertise but my experience. I received lot of support from my Senior and Junior team members and Data migration was successful. 

Alone and we will sink, collectively and we will sail through. 

So, let's begin.

 

  • Negative values allowed for depreciation area.

When you configure asset accounting, Chart of Depreciation area and depreciation area is must. Most important point while configuring Depreciation areas is to enable it for negative values. Once you do this, assets will be allowed for negative posting. 

During our data upload, we uploaded few assets incorrectly. We uploaded without negative values tick mark, and this was creating a problem. System was not allowed to reverse the asset document in AB08. Now all assets’ masters were enabled for negative postings, and it allowed them to reverse, except 1 sub-number asset.

Asset sub-number was deriving values from Main Asset. So, when we check negative value box in Main number, sub number asset error message was reflecting. When we try to check negative value box in Sub Number asset, Main asset error message was reflected.

So always mark negative values for all depreciation areas to avoid such problems.

 

  • Real and derived areas should have same values.

When you configure the depreciation area, make sure all values in Real areas and Derived areas are similar.  For e.g. 01 and 30. 01 is Real and 30 is derived from 01. In 01, if Acquisition values are ALL VALUES ALLOWED, then 30 should have same values. I know this is very simple and basic, but simples’ things tend to be missed more. So, check all this in detail.

 

  • Sub number asset derived field should be editable.

Generally, we derive values in sub number from Main Asset number, and this is valid too. But those fields are non-editable, and this may create last minute problems. As mentioned above, we tried to reverse the Asset and Sub Asset, but it was not allowed to reverse due to negative postings. Also, it was not allowed to save both the assets after checking Negative values Box.

Reason, in Asset main number, depreciation key was 0001 and in sub asset number it was 0001. We wanted to change to 0002 but field was not modified. If field would be editable, this problem would have been mitigated.

Go to AO21. Sbno. Should be ticked.

 

  • All Depreciation area in asset master

Depreciation area configured in OADB should reflect in Asset master in AS03. If it is not reflected, it is a concern. I know this is basic, but this can be missed.  Check for all company codes why this depreciation area is not coming. Because moving transport at the last moment may create lot of problems. If you do not see the depreciation area, go to OAYZ and check if it is deactivated. If yes, activate.

Note : In ECC, there was AFAN tcode to activate all depreciation area but I could not found this in S/4. We need to activate one by one for each Asset Class.

 

  • Real Depreciation Area Tick Mark.

When configuration is done, check Real area tick mark is made only for Real Depreciation Area. For derived depreciation area there should be no tick mark. If you try to change this at later point of time, this will create problem. So do check this beforehand.

 

  • Data templates should have negative values allowed.

When data is uploaded, instruct your Data team to put X in negative values box for all Asset master’s file. With this, your asset master is now enabled for negative postings. This will help you at the time of reversal of asset.

 

  • Bot should be configured for exact date.

This could be a big issue for Data Migration team. For e.g. extraction date is 31.12.2025. so you should get data as on 31.12.2025. Cumulative value should have data, but depreciation posted and Transaction data should be blank.

Now what happens, BOT is not configured/customized to extract data for exact date, and this could be a problem.  For e.g. you are on 18-02-2026. Data to be extracted as on 31.12.2025 but BOT is not customized to extract data as on 31.12.2025. So, they will extract 3 sheets for you.

  1. Cumulative value
  2. Depreciation posted
  3. Transaction data.

Data team will spend time in collating above information and there is a big risk, like data mismatch.

 

  •      Data Re-upload

Always be ready for this. Sometimes 

  1. cost center is incorrect or
  2. sub number field is not editable or
  3. Depreciation key is incorrect or
  4. Special Depreciation is not reflecting in AW01N or Asset History sheet. 
  5. ......Could be many more.

 

  •     Data Verification.

Sometime client complaints about data is incorrect. They are completely mismatching and you start spending time in reconciliation. At the end you find all data upload is correct and there is no error. So where is the error? 

Ask the user which file you have referred? Extraction file or converted file? Sometimes they reconcile data with the Extracted file from legacy system and not the converted file. This seems very simple, but this will take away lot of time.

 

  •    Data conversion (Most Important)

Never take responsibility of file conversion from the client. Tell client to convert the raw data to uploaded data and share with us to upload. If you take this responsibility, this will create many problems like - 

  1. Time management
  2. Erroneous data
  3. Accountable and responsible.
  4. ......could be many more.

 

  •     Access to legacy system

Make sure you have access to legacy system if you are responsible for data conversion. This will help you verify the data upfront with extracted file.  If you do not have access, you will have dependency upon user to extract data from legacy system. This is very tiring and cumbersome.

So to conclude, there are many more things in data migration which need to be taken care. Above points are one which i fell are important, so shared with you all.

Hope you liked this blog.

Regards

CA Zunaid Hingora

entry.title: How Do I Activate a Situation Type in the Extended Framework?
entry.content:
Situation Handling notifies users about issues requiring their attention. You may choose SAP delivered situation templates or design your own use cases. For the extended framework for Situation Handling, we often discussed how you can create custom situation templates. The framework comes also with SAP delivered use cases that you can easily activate.

This blog post explains how you can activate situation types from SAP delivered situation templates with the extended framework for Situation Handling. It has been available since SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2202 and SAP S/4HANA 2021 FPS02.

 

SAP Delivered Use Cases

Looking for Situation Handling use cases? Visit SAP Help and navigate to Situation Handling, then choose Use Cases. You can narrow your results by filtering for Situation Template Type (either extended object-based or message-based), area, and topic. To learn more about a specific use case, simply check the link in the Situation Template column for a detailed description.

Use Case

For illustration purposes we use the situation template Expiration of Purchase Contracts (PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR). Purchasers get a heads-up for purchase contracts that are about to expire.

 

Activation Overview for 2SL and 3SL

Before discussing the activation steps in detail, let me point out the differences between system landscapes and their specific transport paths.

In the extended framework for Situation Handling, you create a situation type from a situation template. Situation templates are only visible in the quality system of a 2-system landscape (2SL) and the development system of a  3-system-landscape (3SL). The situation type needs to be transported to the production system. The transport paths differ between 2SL and 3SL.

Transport the Situation Type in 2SL

In a 2-system-landscape, you transport the situation type from the quality system to the production system.

  • In the quality system you
    • Create a situation type from a situation template
    • Test if the configuration alerts the right users about the situation
    • Export the well-defined situation type
  • In the production system you
    • Import the situation type
    • Since the data usually differs between quality and production system you need to enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Enable the situation type

Transports in 2-System-Landscape

Transport the Situation Type in 3SL

In a 3-system-landscape, transports are required from the development system to both the test and production system. In a 3SL environment you may also have different tenants for development, customizing, and parallel customizing that need to be considered as well.

  • On the customizing tenant in the development system you
    • Create a situation type from a situation template
    • Export the situation type
  • On the test tenant in the test system you
    • Import the situation type
    • Since the data usually differs between systems you need to enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Test if the configuration alerts the right users about the situation
    • Forward the well-defined situation type
  • On the production tenant in the production system you
    • Import the forwarded situation type
    • Enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Enable the situation type

Transports in 3-System-Landscape

Detailed Activation of a Situation Type in 3SL

Now let’s check the detailed steps for a sample use case in a 3-system-landscape as it is more comprehensive. In 2SL the export / import mechanism resembles the one from development system to test system in 3SL.

Depending on your system setting, the transports may also be processed automatically. For optimal clarity, the blog post discusses the transport steps through manual triggers.

Development System

Create a Situation Type from a Situation Template

Log-in as business process configuration expert on the customizing tenant in the development system. Open the Manage Situation Types – Extended app where you see a list of SAP delivered templates. Use the search option to find the template for our use case Expiration of Purchase Contracts (PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR). Select the template and an object page opens. In the header, select Create Situation Type.

Create situation type from situation template

A new page opens with the situation type that contains all information from the situation template. You just need to enter an ID, Name and Description. The type is configured and ready to use. Depending on your requirements you could adapt the settings, for example, by adding filter values for the conditions, changing texts or rescheduling the batch job.

I recommend monitoring the instances to see how situations are handled.

Monitor situations

Let’s check the relevant settings and understand how the configuration works.

There are two Situation Triggers that detect situations and cover different usage scenarios. While the batch-based trigger checks for situations in the background, the event-based trigger reacts on every active change of the contract.

Situation triggers

The situation triggers cover the following cases:

  • The Event-Based Trigger checks the conditions whenever you create or update a purchase contract.
    • Condition 1 checks if the validity period of a purchase contract expires within the next 30 days and triggers a situation. The information displayed to the user is defined in Situation Display number 1.
    • Condition 2 is only executed if condition 1 did not match. That is, the validity period is not within the next 30 days, for instance when the contract got extended, and the situation is closed. As nobody will be informed about the closure, the texts are empty (Situation Display number 2).

Event-based trigger

  • In addition to the events, the Batch-Based Trigger regularly checks the validity end date according to the batch schedule.
    • Condition 1 checks if the validity period of a purchase contract expires within the next 30 days and triggers a situation.

Batch-based trigger

The configuration for the Batch Job Scheduling specifies that the contract dates are verified every 3 hours throughout the day, beginning at 6:00 a.m.

Batch job scheduling

Export the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator on the customizing tenant in the development system. Open the Export Software Collection app.

  • On top of the table choose Create Software Collection and a new page opens
  • Choose Add Items and select the situation type you created
  • On the header select Check and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Export and wait until the export is finished successfully

Test System

Import the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator on the test tenant in the test system. Open the Import Collection app.

  • Choose your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Manual Import and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Import on the bottom and wait until the import is finished successfully

Test the Situation Type

As business process specialist you can test the situation type on the corresponding tenant.

Make sure that the situation type is enabled.

Activate the situation type

Then maintain the conditions. The filter values are not transported since they may differ in the various systems and tenants.

  • Select the Event-Based Trigger and a new page opens
    • Condition 1 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
      • Validity Period End: enter Next30Days, using the value help
    • Condition 2 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
    • Go back to the main page

Event-based trigger

  • Select the Batch-Based Trigger and a new page opens
    • Condition 1 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
      • Validity Period End: enter Next30Days, using the value help
    • Go back to the main page

Batch-based trigger

With the situation triggers, you defined when a situation is detected. Now, you need to define who should be informed about situations. Maintain the team(s) in the Manage Teams and Responsibilities app and ensure that at least one team is enabled. The filter settings work the same way as with the standard framework for Situation Handling.

Situation recipients

Now you can test the situation type in your business area with the corresponding application data. You can check if there are purchase contracts that match the conditions by choosing Trigger Batch and Manage Instances.

Test the type with trigger batch

If no situations were detected you can log-in as purchaser and create or manipulate a purchasing contract. Add an end date within the next 30 days and the save event will trigger a situation.

When you log-in as a team member of the recipients team you can also check, if you were alerted about the situation.

After the successful test you push the situation type to the production system.

Forward the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator and open the Import Collection app.

  • Select your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Forward and wait until the validation is processed successfully
  • Then choose Forward on the bottom and wait until the export is finished successfully

Production System

Import Collection to Production System

As administrator open the Import Collection app on the production tenant in the production system.

  • Choose your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Manual Import and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Import on the bottom and wait until the import is finished successfully

Activate the Situation Type

Log-in as business process specialist and open the Manage Situation Types – Extended app. Like in the test system, you need to maintain the situation type.

  • Enable the situation type
  • Add the filter values for the trigger conditions
  • Ensure that at least one recipient team is enabled

Now you are all set.

Further Information

You find many more blog posts in the Best Practices section of Intelligent Situation Handling in the SAP Community. And you can visit SAP Help Portal for further guidance on Situation Handling.

entry.title: Influence Tomorrow’s ERP Technology with SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1-2026
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA is transforming the way businesses operate. By delivering intelligent automation, real-time analytics, and seamless integration across processes, it enables organizations to drive efficiency, agility, and innovation at scale. Now, you can help shape the future of enterprise resource planning.

Cycle 1 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is still open, offering SAP customers and partners the unique opportunity to directly influence the evolution of SAP S/4HANA. Collaborate with SAP product teams, share your insights early, and contribute to the development of new features and enhancements.

This is more than just a preview – it’s your chance to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of intelligent ERP solutions.

Registration is open until March 13, 2026. 

Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

ERP Cloud Delivery & Excellence 

  • A modern Output Management experience in ERP Suite
  • AI Assisted SAP Workflow Management
  • AI assisted use cases for Embedded Analytics
  • Business Partner and Product Maser in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master Data Analytics Dashboard
  • Task Center (One Inbox) experience evaluation in SAP Business Suite

ERP - Cross Topics

  • Farmer Collaboration and Engagement

ERP Finance & Risk

  • Improving Sanction Party List screening with AI
  • Limit Management Modernization (Credit Risk Analyzer)
  • Simplify and Improve the Intrastat Declaration Process with AI
  • Unlock more flexible Margin Analysis in SAP Cloud ERP

ERP Private Cloud

  • Embedded AI and Joule for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • E-Mails in business context with integrated business communications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

ERP Public Cloud

  • Agentic AI – Analytical Business Insights
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI Agent with Financial Correspondences
  • Concept for Harmonized Period Control
  • Customer returns user experience
  • Configuration experience of Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Enhanced Payment Order Management in Automatic Payment Run Process
  • Identity and Access Management: Upgrade and Change Handling
  • IFRS 9- Credit-Risk Based Impairment for Trade Receivables using Advanced Valuation
  • Generate Settlement Rules
  • Parallel Unit of Measure
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies & provide smart staffing across different roles of projects

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Your Blueprint to Lead the Shift in SAP S/4HANA Utilities
entry.content:
The Utilities industry is moving fast toward cloud-ready architectures, customer-first operations, and intelligent,  AI-enabled processes. If you're an architect, business user , or consultant, you're at the center of this transformation.  

The journey begins with a thorough introduction to SAP S/4HANA Utilities, covering unique system capabilities, end-to-end industry processes, and customer service operations. From there, you will explore the strategic differences between SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA Utilities, learning to evaluate conversion options and apply proven transition methodologies using tools like SAP Readiness Check. 

The curriculum then guides you through essential system configuration, including master data modeling, device management with inventory and procurement processes, meter reading, and integration with other SAP components. Billing and invoicing are addressed in depth, from rate structure configuration to budget billing plans and exception handling. 

For the German energy market, specialized training covers market communication processes, including the cloud-based SAP Market Communication (MaCo) solution, Application Process Engine (APE), and the integration into SAP S/4HANA Utilities with a focus on regulatory compliance and automation. 

Finally, the curriculum prepares you to build future-proof architectures by keeping the SAP S/4HANA core clean through extensibility on SAP Business Technology Platform, and utilizing SAP Fiori for a modern, industry-specific user experience. 

Ready to go hands on? 

Build end-to-end skills with the refreshed course stack:  

  1. S4IUT is your overview and entry point to the SAP S/4HANA for Utilities. The course covers  the business processes , Fiori-based functions, AI-enabled optimization, asset management architecture, and cloud integrations: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/executing-business-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-utilities 
  2. IUT21 focuses on master data and basic functions configuration: https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-master-data-and-basic-functions-in-sap-s-4hana-utilities 
  3. IUT22 deep-dives into device management configuration, from master data and inventory to installations, inspections, and meter reading: https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-device-management-in-sap-s-4hana-utilities 
  4. IUT23 covers billing and invoicing configuration, including rate structures, execution, exceptions, and budget billing: https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-billing-and-invoicing-in-sap-s-4hana-utilities 
  5. If you operate in the German energy market, IUT51 explores market communication (MaCo-Cloud) with the Application Process Engine (APE): https://learning.sap.com/courses/explore-sap-market-communication-for-utilities-in-the-german-energy-market 
  6. Round it out with the updated S4IUD gives you the strategic and technical insights to guide customers from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA with confidence: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/transitioning-to-sap-s-4hana-for-utilities 

Ready to lead your customers’ transition with clarity and impact? Start with S4IUT (or S4IUD), then progress through the updated stack to build execution and configuration expertise.

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone: Tile Opens(content federation) but App is opening blank in Mobile
entry.content:
I am currently integrating a custom role and tile from an on‑premise S/4HANA system into SAP Build Work Zone using the CDM-based content federation (Exposure Version 2).

Everything works fine in Build Work Zone in the browser.
However, when I scan the QR code in SAP Start mobile app, the tile appears correctly — but after tapping the tile, the app opens a blank white screen.

 

 

Has anyone experienced this issue where a tile loads correctly in Build Work Zone, but shows a blank screen when launched from SAP Start?

entry.title: Old Tile Still Appearing in SAP Build Work Zone After Complete Re‑Exposure from S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am facing an issue with federated content from an on‑premise S/4HANA system to SAP Build Work Zone (Standard Edition).
Our authorization team has removed the old tile and assigned a new tile to the correct custom role, custom catalog, and custom group in S/4HANA.

To ensure a clean setup, I deleted the previous exposure configuration and re‑created the full setup from scratch in:

  • /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE (content exposure)
  • SAP BTP → Build Work Zone → Content Provider → Synchronize Content

Despite repeating the full process multiple times, the old tile continues to appear in Build Work Zone instead of the new one.
The authorization consultant has verified both the new role and the tile assignments several times on the S/4HANA side, and everything looks correct there — but Build Work Zone keeps federating only the old tile.

Has anyone experienced something similar?

I am trying to understand:

  • Why outdated tile information is still being federated
  • Whether additional cleanup is required on the CDM or content provider side
  • If there are any cache layers (S/4HANA, BTP, or Build Work Zone) that must be explicitly cleared
  • Whether Exposure Version 2 requires specific steps to refresh or replace previously published app IDs

Any advice, known issues, or guidance on how to force Build Work Zone to pick up the updated tile would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Creating a DQM Validation Rule for Business Partner Role Assignment in SAP MDG
entry.content:
Overview

Data quality is critical in Master Data Governance (MDG), especially when dealing with Business Partners. One common data quality issue occurs when Business Partners are created without any Business Partner Role assigned. Since roles determine how a Business Partner participates in business processes (customer, supplier, etc.), missing roles can break downstream applications.

The goal is to create a DQM validation rule in SAP Master Data Governance that prevents the creation or modification of Business Partners without role assignments. The system will show a validation error or warning as shown in the following screenshot:

The validation logic works as follows:

  1. The rule iterates over Business Partner records in table BUT000.
  2. For each Business Partner ID, a table operation checks table BUT100.
  3. If no entry exists in BUT100 for that BP, the validation rule raises an error.

This ensures that every Business Partner has at least one role assigned before it can be saved.

Prerequisites

Before implementing the rule, ensure the following requirements are met:

  • SAP Master Data Governance with Data Quality Management (DQM) enabled
  • User authorization with the SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_STEWARD role or equivalent
  • Access to the SAP Fiori application Validation Rules for Business Partners (App IDs F4084 or F4082A (newer releases)

Rule Configuration

Step 1 — Access the DQM Validation App

  1. Open the SAP Fiori Launchpad.
  2. Launch the Validation Rules for Business Partners app.

This UI/App allows you to manage and create validation rules for Business Partner data

Step 2 — Create a New Validation Rule

Click Create and enter the following information:

Field

Value

Rule ID

CHECK_BP_ROLE_MANDATORY

Rule Name

BP Role Assignment Required

Base Table

BUT000 (Business Partner General Data)

Checked Field

BUT0BK-PARTNER_ROLE (optional but recommended)

The base table must be BUT000, even though the validation checks data stored in another table

Step 3 — Maintain General Information

Fill in the business metadata for the rule:

Description
Ensures every Business Partner has at least one role assigned.

Reason
Business Partners without roles cannot participate in business processes.

Rule Owner
Data Steward responsible for Business Partner data.

Implementation Expert
Technical contact responsible for BRF+ implementation.

Save the rule by clicking Create.

Step 4 — Technical Implementation

Configure Rule Usage

  1. In the Usage section, select Add.
  2. Choose Check in Central Governance.
  3. Click Prepare to generate the BRF+ application.
  4. Select Send for Implementation.

This enables the rule logic to be edited.

Scope Expression Configuration

The Scope Expression defines which Business Partners should be validated.

Steps:

  1. Open the Scope Expression link.
  2. The Simplified BRF+ Workbench appears.
  3. Define the scope.

Example scope:

Partner Category = 2 (Organization)

This means the validation rule will only apply to organizational Business Partners.

You can also configure the rule to apply to all Business Partners.

Condition Expression Implementation

The main validation logic is defined in the Condition Expression.

Steps:

  1. Open the Condition Expression.
  2. Remove the dummy condition.
  3. Create a new expression.

Choose:

Expression Type: Table Operation

Example name: BUT100_CHECK

Table Operation Configuration

Configure the operation as follows:

Parameter

Value

Operation

Has at least

Table

Procedure Call

Procedure

BUT0BK lookup ALL

Condition

Partner Role is not initial

This configuration checks if at least one role exists for the Business Partner.

 

Although the rule loops over BUT000, the lookup ultimately checks role entries in BUT100.

This cross-table validation is the key technical detail of this solution.

Activation and Deployment

Once testing is complete:

  1. Select Send for Testing to change the rule status.
  2. After validation, choose Approve.
  3. Go to the Usage tab.
  4. Click Enable for Check in Central Governance.

The rule is now active and will validate Business Partner data during governance processes.

Testing the Rule

Test the validation logic.

Test Scenarios

Scenario

Expected Result

BP without roles

Validation error

BP with one role

Validation passes

BP with multiple roles

Validation passes

Testing ensures that only valid Business Partner records can be saved.

 

Troubleshooting

Rule Not Triggering

Check the following:

  • All BRF+ expressions are active
  • Rule status is Approved
  • Usage is Enabled

Performance Issues

If validation becomes slow:

  • Restrict the Scope Expression
  • Limit evaluation to specific Business Partner categories
  • Avoid unnecessary table operations

 

Best Regards

Steffen

entry.title: Pregúntame lo que quieras con SAP Champions – Serie mensual (Sesión 2)
entry.content:
Hola querida SAP Community,

Mi nombre es María Antonieta López, SAP Champion de México.

Actualmente, trabajo como Directora de Programas SAP, enfocada en programas globales de transformación SAP, implementaciones de SAP S/4HANA, arquitectura empresarial, marcos de gobernanza e innovación digital dentro del ecosistema SAP.

Como parte de los SAP Champions que lideran la iniciativa “Ask Me Anything” junto con Sebastiano Marchesini,

Me complace informarles que yo estaré liderando la edición de marzo.🚀

Así que sí…

Pueden preguntarme. LO QUE SEA.

 

Después de más de 20 años viviendo dentro de transformaciones SAP, probablemente he visto lo bueno, lo malo… y esos momentos de “¿cómo sobrevivió este sistema al go-live?” 😅

 

Durante las próximas semanas estaré respondiendo cualquier pregunta que tengan — sin filtros — solo SAP en vivo y real.

💡Podemos explorar temas como:

⚙️Implementaciones de SAP S/4HANA y programas globales de transformación – Ejecución de implementaciones end-to-end de SAP S/4HANA y programas de transformación a gran escala, permitiendo a las organizaciones modernizar sus procesos de negocio centrales, estandarizar operaciones globales y adoptar capacidades digitales de nueva generación.

🏗️ Arquitectura Empresarial – Procesos, artefactos y estrategias para la arquitectura empresarial de SAP basada en TOGAF.

🧼 Clean Core en SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public) — procesos de gestión de proyectos para implementar o crear extensiones o interfaces en tu proyecto.

(Hablando de BUILD para configuradores y su uso con Joule, APIs preconstruidas, APIs nuevas, y por supuesto nuestra plataforma como servicio SAP Integration Suite, todo dentro de nuestra querida SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)).

☁️ RISE o GROW con SAP + Gobernanza y metodología Clean Core

Desde SAP Activate hasta marcos como SAP Application Extension Methodology, ISA-M (Integration Solution Advisory Methodology), SAP Design Thinking, SAP Data Architecture Framework y, por qué no… incluso SAP Business AI Framework.

Puedes preguntar sobre qué artefactos usar, cómo ejecutar los procesos o por qué tengo que hacer esta tarea... Y te diré lo que pasará si te los saltas... Muahaha! …💀 así que mis historias de terror, como el invierno… están por llegar.

🏛️ Cómocrear un SAP Center of Excellence (CoE) o brindar servicios de SAP AM (Application Management) y realmente vivir felices para siempre con SAP usando Cloud ALM + Focused Build + Activate.

🌍 Globalización en SAP, ya sea de forma general o por país. ¡Larga vida a SAP Document & Reporting Compliance!

Y también a las extensiones DRC de nuestros partners SAP.

🧭 Roadmaps de soluciones y estrategia de transformación, etc

¿Y cualquier otra cosa? y ... ¡si no sé la respuesta! 👉 Prometo encontrar a alguien que sí la sepa… para ustedes.

 

Ya tenemos todo listo. 💬 Ahora es tu turno.

Deja tu pregunta en la sección de comentario👇 o puedes preguntar de forma anónima aquí 👉 AMA Champion Anonymously Questions o escaneando el QR Code 

 

📅Esta edición del AMA estará abierta para preguntas hasta el 27 de marzo de 2026.

Después de esa fecha, las preguntas seleccionadas serán respondidas en un video grabado que se publicará en el canal de YouTube de la SAP Community  👉 @SAPCommunity

También se publicará un blog resumen con las preguntas y respuestas para la comunidad.

 

Cuéntame…

🤓¿Qué tema de SAP te gustaría que exploremos juntos?

entry.title: Clean Core Is a Leadership Discipline, Not a Technical Rule
entry.content:
I recently took an SAP course about Clean Core, and it left me thinking about how people interpret the concept in practice. Most explanations focus on ways to keep the system as simple as possible, avoiding changing the original software too much. Personally, what we keep seeing in real SAP transformations that our clients bring to our table is that Clean Core doesn’t necessarily represent an issue because of architecture, but rather because of people. 

The tension around Clean Core becomes apparent the moment a decision maker asks to adjust something “just a little”, explains that their processes work a bit differently, or insists that there’s only one way things have always been done. Of course, there are several ways to solve the request, but my question is: Should the change be implemented at all in the first place? And here is where our concern starts, because this is not a technical decision, but a leadership one.

Clean Core Is Not About Restricting the Business

Clean Core is sometimes interpreted as saying “no” to the business, as if it were about restricting flexibility or forcing standardization just because. In reality, it has more to do with protecting the future of the system. With the migration to cloud ERP and faster upgrade cycles, customization starts to play a different role. Things that used to be manageable in traditional on-premise systems can become way more complicated in a cloud environment. Besides the problems of costs or upgrades, customizations introduce a deeper issue: it becomes less clear who is responsible for what.

This became clear after assessing many of our clients’ landscapes, where heavily customized processes often resulted in unclear ownership. Who owns the process: the business or IT? Who decides when and what needs to change? Over time, these customizations tend to end up in a strange situation where no one really owns, questions or removes them. 

Clean Core introduces a different kind of conversation before all those issues come up. It pushes organizations to ask whether a customization is necessary at all: Does it really create value or is it just the protection of a legacy habit? Once again, we are talking about leadership decisions that have almost nothing to do with architecture. 

What Pressure to Customize Often Reveals

Across multiple client transformations, what the strongest pressure to customize reveals are organizational dynamics. Sometimes, there are governance gaps, where decisions are made without a clear framework or roadmap. Other times, as mentioned earlier, ownership is unclear and different teams defend their own variations of a process. In other cases, deadlines and internal pressure push teams towards quick fixes as temporary solutions instead of redesigning processes. Finally, sometimes customization simply reflects resistance to change. 

These dynamics don’t disappear just because we introduce better extensibility tools. They tend to improve only when leadership alignment improves, and Clean Core works when teams are clear about when it actually makes sense to move away from standard. The key is being intentional. In the projects we’ve worked on, the teams that manage this better are the ones that pause and ask themselves simple questions like if a customization will create problems later on or if it would make upgrades harder. 

Our Experience

Recently, we worked on a S/4 project for a company that had started using drones to collect field data for forecasting. The goal was to move beyond manual reports and gain more real-time visibility from physical assets. Of course, Clean Core was part of the strategy from the beginning, as the goal was to keep S/4 standard and integrate new capabilities in a sustainable way.

Once the workshops started, the situation became more complicated. The forecasting team suggested adding custom fields in inventory and maintenance documents so the drone data could be stored directly in the S/4 tables. It wasn’t an unreasonable idea, we understood that they just wanted the data where everything else already lived.

With that idea on the table, the conversation started moving forward. If the drone data was already in S/4, why not use it directly in the planning logic? There was even the suggestion of modifying the material planning process so the drone data could automatically adjust demand forecasts. What they were asking for was technically possible, and thinking back on, it would’ve probably been faster to just build it directly in the core. This was exactly the moment where all I’ve stated about Clean Core became a reality for us, we had no other choice but to ask ourselves if this logic should really live inside the S/4 core.

In the end, we decided to keep the drone data outside of S/4 and bring the results back through standard interfaces. Yes, it took a bit more coordination, but the core system stayed clean. 

What that project made clear to us is how these situations usually start. At the beginning everyone agrees with the idea of keeping the system standard. But once the workshops begin, small requests start appearing. None of them sound dramatic and, again, they are technically possible and even make sense. But that’s usually how heavy customization slowly creeps back into the system.

Clean Core as Cultural Discipline

In the end, Clean Core says a lot about how an organization makes decisions. In our experience, it depends less on tools and more on leadership, ownership, and how teams make decisions during a transformation. Staying “clean” is not about having a perfect system, but about pausing before customizing and asking whether the change is truly necessary. 

When Clean Core breaks down, it is rarely because the technology couldn’t support the extension. More often it happens when ownership is unclear or short-term pressure overrides long-term thinking. In that sense, Clean Core ends up revealing how disciplined an organization really is when making technology decisions.

entry.title: Build Partner Add-on with IDE Action
entry.content:
Disclaimer: The namespace /ITAPC3/ is used throughout this document as an example namespace only. Replace it with your own namespace in real implementations. The coding style/naming conventions used in this blog are not recommendations on naming/design 
  
This blog post covers a step-by-step guide to how partners implement IDE Actions as Partner Add-ons in the SAP BTP ABAP Environment. This enables SAP partners to deliver customized tools in the form of custom action in ABAP Development Tools that increase developer productivity. By combining IDE Actions with the installable add-on option of the BTP ABAP Environment, partners have a potentially monetizable option to extend the ABAP Developer tools. It covers the key concepts and technologies – how to extend ABAP Development Tools with IDE Actions and how to leverage the SAP Business Technology Platform ABAP Environment alongside the Landscape Portal to deliver installable add-on products. 

Here we will look into a partner persona, where they can provide the business solution to their consumer with an installable add-on by providing certain action in the ABAP development tools, and also look into the consumer side of how they can consume the solution which caters to the needs of their developer using ABAP development tools (ADT). 

Why IDE actions and Installable add-ons? 

By combining IDE actions and installable add-ons, partners who have been extending software can now also extend the ABAP Development Tools (ADT). 

  • The IDE action framework allows extending the ABAP development tools (ADT) with custom server-driven context-aware actions. IDE Actions allow partners to automate repetitive tasks, such as creating specialized development objects, enhancing code analysis, or automating some code generation within ABAP Development Tools (ADT) for Eclipse. The implementation is completely server-driven using ABAP only. You can also use these custom functionalities with AI integration and XCO libraries. 
  • Installable add-ons are a set of software components bundled together as an add-on by a partner with the intent of providing a solution to their customers.  
  • The Landscape Portal acts as a central tool to allow service providers to perform administration tasks such as hibernating systems, creating test tenants and support users, selecting systems for a pre-upgrade, but also lifecycle management operations such as building, deploying and updating product versions, and even guides you through the configuration and deployment of multitenant SaaS solutions. 

 Business scenario: 

The developer can document development objects in the Knowledge Transfer Document Editor using Markdown syntax. Here partner is offering a translation action with the help of IDE Action as Add-on for the Knowledge Transfer Document and can use the ABAP AI SDK powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) to translate the Markdown syntax and display the output of translated Knowledge Transfer Document. Consumer (The partner's customer) can consume this solution from the partner through landscape portal as Installable add-on and can use customized action in ABAP Development Tools for their development scenario. 

Prerequisites: (Partner) 

  • You’ve purchased entitlements that are necessary for the account setup. See Prepare 
  • You’ve registered a namespace at SAP, for example /NAMESPC/. See Register a Namespace 
  • You’ve registered your add-on at SAP, for example /NAMESPC/PRODUCTX. See Register a Product 
  • You are subscribed to the Landscape Portal 
  • You have a user with the Landscape Portal Admin role collection assigned 
  • Install ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT) on your computer. If you already have ADT, make sure it's updated to the latest version. For more information, see ABAP Development Tools. 
  • You need to have access to an SAP BTP ABAP Environment Instance 
  • Business Catalog SAP_A4C_BC_DEV_AIA_PC required for creating and editing IDE actions 

 Prerequisites: (Consumer) 

  • The Landscape Portal Application has been subscribed to and your user has the landscape portal admin role collection assigned 
  • Install ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT) on your computer. If you already have ADT, make sure it's updated to the latest version. For more information, see ABAP Development Tools. 

 Steps to Implement as a Partner 

1. Create Software Components 

  • To open the "Manage Software Components" application, log in to your SAP BTP ABAP Environment Fiori Launchpad as an administrator and Search for "Manage Software Components" in the search bar. 
  • use the “Create” button in the “Manage Software Components” app to open the dialog and fill in the information. We enter the name of the software component and a description. In the namespace dropdown you can use your reserved namespace. (Disclaimer: /ITAPC3/ being an example namespace used in this blog) 

  • You clone the software component into the development system. When cloning, the main branch is checked out for the system, a package is created in the system that has the same name as the software component and is also assigned to it. At the same time, you can also set how the clone of the repository behaves in the system (push/pull or just pull). 
  • The software component with the selected branch is now imported into your current system instance. The Clone button turns into a Pull button. From now on, you can use it to pull (remotely available) changes of your software component to your system. Now you can see package under the selected software component in your system. 

2.  Development (Intelligent Scenario) 

Here in this scenario, we would be using ABAP AI Capabilities is our IDE Action implementation class to translate the markdown content into the target language with the help of ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM. To learn more about Developing AI Enabled Applications with ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM, refer to the blog post. 

2.1 Create ISLM Scenario: 

  • Select your package, right-click, New → Other → Intelligent Scenario → /ITAPC3/ADT_INT_SCE 

Enter a name and description. Select SIDEBYSIDE for Scenario Technology field. Choose Next and then click Finish. 

2.2 Create ISLM Model: 

  • Select your package, right-click, New → Other → Intelligent Scenario Model 
  • Fill the Description and Model Name fields. Assign the previously created scenario in Intelligent Scenario Name. Choose Next.  

  • Enter the ExecutableID and Large Language Model Name and Large Language Model Version and Activate the object. 

3.  Development (IDE Action) 

In this section we will look at the IDE action which translates the markdown content into the selected language with the help of intelligent scenario created in the previous step. 

Partners can utilize the ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM to embed Generative AI features in IDE action implementation class. 

  • From ABAP Development Tools, locate and add to the favorite package “/ITAPC3/IDE_ACTION_EXTENSIONS“ that was created during Create Software Components process. The package has the same name as the software component. 
  • Create a new IDE action either from the main menu, project explorer, or toolbar. 
  • In the main menu, choose File->New->Other... and select IDE Action. The creation wizard opens. 
  • In the creation wizard, enter the project and package details if they are not already filled. Also, provide a name and description for the IDE action. 
  • Choose Next, assign a transport request if needed and Choose Finish. 

  • After we have created the action in the system, we then fill the form with the relevant information to create the action and the Input UI Configuration Class. In the Filter: Cardinality section, decide the number of focused resources that can be selected when running an IDE action. If you want to use your IDE action for any object type, add *ALL as value for the object type. Save and activate your IDE action. 

 

3.1 Defining UI 

  • In the first step we need an action dialog to enable the user to enter data. To do this we can create a new class using the link in the IDE Action. This implements the interface IF_AIA_SD_ACTION_INPUT, but only the method CREATE_INPUT_CONFIG is defined here; the other three methods can be implemented optionally. We define a public type in the class as the structure for the input; we can give the fields certain properties using the ABAP Docs. A list of properties can be found in the documentation, with certain properties being set via configuration. 

 

CLASS /itapc3/cl_trans_ui_info DEFINITION 
  PUBLIC 
  FINAL 
  CREATE PUBLIC . 
  PUBLIC SECTION. 
  INTERFACES if_aia_sd_action_input. 
     "! <p class="shorttext">Simple Controls</p> 
      BEGIN OF ty_input, 
      "! <p class="shorttext">Select the language</p> 
      custom_value_help_text_field TYPE string, 
      "! <p class="shorttext">Enter the markdown source for the translation</p> 
        multiline_text_field         TYPE string, 
      END OF ty_input. 
  PROTECTED SECTION. 
  PRIVATE SECTION. 
ENDCLASS. 

CLASS /ITAPC3/CL_TRANS_UI_INFO IMPLEMENTATION. 
  METHOD if_aia_sd_action_input~create_input_config. 
  " Create the input data with default values 
  DATA(input_data) = VALUE ty_input( ) ##NO_TEXT. 
  " Create the configuration via the factory 
  DATA(configuration) = ui_information_factory>get_configuration_factory( 
                                       )->create_for_data( input_data ). 
  " Set the layout 
  configuration->set_layout( if_sd_config_element=>layout-grid ). 
  " Set configuration for elements 
  “To enable a custom value help for an element, the corresponding configuration must be set via the API: 
   configuration->get_element( 'custom_value_help_text_field' )->set_values( if_sd_config_element=>values_kind-domain_specific_named_items ). 
    configuration->get_element( 'multiline_text_field' )->set_multiline( if_sd_config_element=>height-large ). 
  " Create and return the UI information 
 result = ui_information_factory->for_abap_type( abap_type     = input_data 
             configuration = configuration ). 
 ENDMETHOD. 
 METHOD if_aia_sd_action_input~get_value_help_provider. 
  " Return a reference to your value help handler via the value help provider 
   result = cl_sd_value_help_provider=>create( NEW /ITAPC3/CL_TRAN_VALUE_HELP( ) ). 
  ENDMETHOD. 
ENDCLASS.

 

3.2 Implement a Custom Value Help: 

  • To implement a custom value help that returns domain-specific named items, a handler class must be provided that implements the if_sd_value_help_dsni interface. The get_value_help_items method returns the values for the value help. The values to be returned are of type if_sd_value_help_dsni=>ty_named_item_list. 

 

CLASS /itapc3/cl_trans_ide_action DEFINITION 
  PUBLIC 
  FINAL 
  CREATE PUBLIC. 
  PUBLIC SECTION. 
  INTERFACES if_aia_action. 
  PROTECTED SECTION. 
  PRIVATE SECTION. 
  METHODS get_input_as_html 
      IMPORTING ui_input      TYPE /itapc3/cl_trans_ui_info=>ty_input 
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE string. 
ENDCLASS. 

CLASS /ITAPC3/CL_TRANS_IDE_ACTION IMPLEMENTATION. 
 METHOD get_input_as_html. 
    FINAL(nl) = cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline. 
    TRY. 
        DATA(completion_api) = cl_aic_islm_compl_api_factory=>get( )->create_instance( '/ITAPC3/ADT_INT_SCE' ). 
        DATA(prompt) = |You are a translator. Reject Hate Speech. Translate the given text into { ui_input-custom_value_help_text_field }:{ nl }{ ui_input-multiline_text_field }| ##NO_TEXT. 
        DATA(translation) = completion_api->execute_for_string( prompt )->get_completion( ). 
      CATCH cx_aic_api_factory INTO DATA(aic_api_factory_ex). 
      RETURN. 
      CATCH cx_aic_completion_api INTO DATA(aic_completion_api_ex). 
      RETURN. 
    ENDTRY. 
    result =  '<h3 style="color:gray">Translate to (' && ui_input-custom_value_help_text_field && ')</h3>' && nl  && '<pre>' && translation && '</pre>'. 
  ENDMETHOD. 

  METHOD if_aia_action~run. 
  DATA(focused_resource) = context->get_focused_resource( ). 
    TRY. 
        DATA ui_input TYPE /itapc3/cl_trans_ui_info=>ty_input. 
        context->get_input_config_content( )->get_as_structure( IMPORTING result = ui_input ). 
      CATCH cx_sd_invalid_data INTO DATA(exception). 
        CLEAR ui_input. 
    ENDTRY. 
    DATA(popup_result) = cl_aia_result_factory=>create_html_popup_result( ). 
    popup_result->set_content( get_input_as_html( ui_input ) ). 
    result = popup_result. 
  ENDMETHOD. 
ENDCLASS.

 

To start the action in Eclipse, we can copy the markdown source from any KTD Editor and use  use the shortcut Ctrl + Alt + R for Windows or CMD + OPT + R for Mac or choose Run->ABAP IDE Action... from the main menu to start the Action Dialog, where our IDE Action should now be available. 

Once all the required objects are created and the action is working as per our expectation, we have to release the Transport Request. 

4.  Create Add-on Product using Landscape Portal 

The Landscape Portal acts as a central tool to allow service providers to perform administration tasks such as hibernating systems, creating test tenants and support users, selecting systems for a pre-upgrade, but also lifecycle management operations such as building, deploying and updating product versions, and even guides you through the configuration and deployment of multitenant SaaS solutions. 

Here partner can create a new Add-on product, register your product, build a product version via pipelines and deploy a product version. 

4.1 Register Product 

A product refers to an ABAP add-on that can be installed on SAP BTP, ABAP environment. 

  • Log into the Landscape Portal. For more information, see Landscape Portal   
  • Click on the Register Product tile to open the app. 
  • A list of all your created products, including their product ID, namespace and registration status, is displayed. The registration status indicates whether a product has already been registered to the global account you are currently logged in to. 
  • Click the Create button in the top right corner. 
  • Enter the name of the product you want to create and confirm with Create. Your request for creation has been sent and the registration status of the product changes to “Pending…”. The namespace is automatically determined from the product name you entered, and the product ID is generated as part of the creation process. 

 4.2 Build Product Version 

You can use the Build Product Version app to easily trigger the build of new product versions via pipelines based on templates that you can configure for different use cases such as new (test or production) release deliveries, (test or production) support packages or (test or production) hotfixes/patch deliveries. 

  • Log in to the Landscape Portal and click on the tile Build Product Version. For more information, see Build Product Version 
  • Select one of your products from the list. You will be forwarded to the Product Versions tab. Here, you can see a table listing all the existing versions of this product, their type, when they were created, as well as their delivery status. 
  • You also need to Configure a Pipeline Template for release build. For more information, see Configure a Pipeline Template and Create a template for release build.  
  • To create a new product version, click the Create button, choose Release Delivery and select the desired type. The resulting product version will be calculated automatically. 
  • Select software components to be included in your new product version. To add additional software components, click the Add button on the right. For each software component, define a component version, enter the desired branch and commit ID as seen in the Manage Software Components app, and select in which languages your product version should be built. To get relevant commit ID for the software component involved in the build, you can refer the help page to get more details on this. Identify the commit based on which you wish to build your add-on product. 

  • Click the Build Product Version button to trigger the build of the pipeline for your new product version. 
  • You will be redirected to the Pipeline Status screen, where you can track the progress of your build. 

4.3 Check Product Version 

The Check Product Version app lets you view the results of product version delivery infrastructure checks to see if your product version is ready for delivery.  

  • Click on the Check Product Version tile to open the app. For more information, see Check Product Version 
  • A list of all your products is displayed. Select one of the products from the list. 
  • Ready for production (P): The product version is ready for production use. 

4.4 Publish Product 

You, as a partner, have developed a product that you want to make available to your customers as an add-on that they can install into their system. You can use the Publish Product app to publish your product in a specific version for use by customers. Your customer can then use the Discover Product app to view all product versions that were published, i.e. made available, for them and can register a product, thus adding a product to their pool of product versions available for deployment. 

  1. Log into the Landscape Portal. 
  1. Click the Publish Product tile to open the app. For more information, see Publish Product 
  1. Select the product from the list. You are forwarded to the configuration page. Click the Edit button. 
  1. In the Product Configuration section, select the Product Version that you want to publish and choose SAP Store as the Distribution Channel. 
  1. Click the Publish button to publish your product version 

 

Steps performed from the Consumer 

1. Discover Product 

  • Once Partner has made their product available and has published a product version using the Publish Product app, customers can register the product via Discover Product app. 
  • After registrations customers can deploy the product using Deploy Product.  

2.  Deploy Product 

You can use the Deploy Product app to deploy or update your product version in specific systems. 

  • Sign in to the Landscape Portal. 
  • Click on the Deploy Product tile to open the app. 
  • On the left side, under Product and Product Version Selection, select which product and version you want to deploy or update from the drop-down menu. 
  • In the Available Systems section, select at least one system in which you want to deploy or update the product version. You can also select multiple systems at once. 
  • Click the Deploy button. A dialog box will open. Fill in the remaining details: 
  • Title: Choose a title for your update. 
  • From: Select the time the update can begin. 
  • To: The estimated time at which the product version update will be completed. 
  • Confirm with Schedule. 
  • Check the list under Product Version Update Status. Here, all update requests as well as their current status and progress are displayed. The update you just scheduled has been added to the list. 
  • Once the product is deployed successfully then Customer can access the product in target system. 

SAP BTP automatically activates the software component provided from the partner in the ABAP environment, makes packages visible and registers IDE extensions. 

3.  Test IDE Action 

Create/Use ABAP Cloud project in the ABAP instance where you have deployed the product.This should be a development system. 

Open any Knowledge Transfer Document in the editor and copy the content. 

To open the Run ABAP IDE Action dialog, use the shortcut Ctrl + Alt + R for Windows or CMD + OPT + R for Mac or choose Run->ABAP IDE Action... from the main menu. 

Select the IDE action “Translate markdown content” you would like to run Select Run. 

It will open the action input dialog where you can select the target language you want to translate and in next text box area provide the Knowledge Transfer Document content. 

Select Run. 

The result dialog for the selected IDE action opens the result dialog where it translates the Knowledge Transfer Document to the selected language option from the input dialog.

entry.title: CDS Custom Entities
entry.content:
Introduction

A CDS custom entity is to implement own data retrieval using ABAP.

A CDS custom entity is linked with an ABAP class, and the data retrieval is executed on the application server.

Custom entities are used for data models whose runtime is implemented manually.

At the CDS level, only an interface is defined.

Runtime of a CDS Custom Entity

It shows that the data retrieval is implemented by an ABAP class and the data source does not have to be a database table.

CDS custom entities transient entities, which means they aren't created as a database object and can't be accessed using ABAP SQL.
